solution:

All other reagents are used as reducing agent.

mass of gas used : =29-23=6Kg

gas left =14.2-6=8.2Kg

Consider two statements:

S1 there exists x1,x2∈2,4, x1<x2, such that f'x1=-1 and f'x2=0.

S2 there exists x3,x4∈2,4, x3<x4, such that f is decreasing in 2,x4, increasing in x4,4 and 2f'x3=3fx4 then
solution:
Given:

fx=x3-6x2+ax+b

Given that:

f2=0⇒2a+b=16    ....i

f4=0⇒4a+b=32    ....ii

Solving both equations, we get

a=8, b=0

∴  fx=x3-6x2+8x

⇒  fx=xx-2x-4

Now,

f'x=3x2-12x+8

Now,

If f'x=-1

⇒3x2-12x+8=-1

⇒3x2-12x+9=0

⇒x2-4x+3=0

⇒x2-3x-x+3=0

⇒x-1x-3=0

⇒x=1, 3

Since, x1∈2,4, therefore x1=3.

And,

f'x=0

⇒3x2-12x+8=0

⇒x=12±144-966

⇒x=12±486

⇒x=12±436=6±233

⇒x=2+233, 2-233

Then, 2+233∈2,4

Hence, x2=2+233

So, S1 is true.

Now,

f'x=x-2+233x-2-233

Sign scheme for f'x is as follows:

f'x>0 ∀x∈-∞,2-233∪2+233,∞, hence it is increasing.

f'x<0 ∀x∈2-233,2+233, hence it is decreasing.

So, x4=2+233.

Now,

2f'x3=3fx4

⇒23x32-12x3+8=32+2332+233-22+233-4

⇒23x32-12x3+8=3233+2233233-2

⇒23x32-12x3+8=2129-4

⇒3x32-12x3+8=-83

⇒9x32-36x3+32=0

⇒x3=36±1296-115218=36±1218

⇒x3=83, 43

So, S2 is true.

question: The number of pairs <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> of real numbers, such that whenever <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is a root of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></math> is also a root of this equation, is :
solution:
Consider the equation x2+ax+b=0

It has two roots (not necessarily real α and β)

Either α=β or α≠β

Case (1) If α=β, then it is repeated root. Given than α2-2 is also root

So, α=α2-2

⇒  α+1α-2=0

⇒ α=-1 or α=2

When α=-1 then a,b=2,1

α=2 then a,b=-4,4

Case (2) If α≠β, then four possibilities are there

(I) α=α2-2 and β=β2-2

Here α,β=2,-1 or -1,2

Hence a,b=-α+β,αβ

=-1,-2

(II) α=β2-2 and β=α2-2

Then α-β=β2-α2=β-αβ+α

Since α≠β we get α+β=β2+α2-4

α+β=α+β2-2αβ-4

Thus -1=1-2αβ-4 which implies

αβ=-1. Therefore a,b=-α+β,αβ

=1,-1

(III) α=α2-2=β2-2 and α≠β

⇒ α=-β

Thus α=2,β=-2

α=-1,β=1

Therefore a,b=0,-4 and 0,-1

(IV) β=α2-2=β2-2 and α≠β is same as (III)

Therefore we get 6 pairs of a,b

Which are 2,1,-4,4,-1,-2,1,-1,0,-4,0,-1

question:
The temperature of 3.00 mol of an ideal diatomic gas is increased by 40.0°C without changing the pressure of the gas. The molecules in the gas rotate but do not oscillate. If the ratio of change in internal energy of the gas to the amount of workdone by the gas is x10. Then the value of x (round off to the nearest integer) is _________.

( GivenR=8.31 J mol-1 K-1
solution:
First, recall the formula of change in internal energy in terms of molar specific heat at constant volume and change in temperature, ∆U=nCVΔT, for diatomic gas, CV=f2R=52R, ⇒∆U=n×52×RΔT

⇒ΔUW=52nRΔTnRΔT=52=2510

So, x=25
